name: ButtonProc_FFTpro
description: Panel button callback for FFT, QPI, phase, or lock-in analysis; linecut, slice, or region extraction. Purpose: performs Fourier, filtering, phase, or lock-in processing. Usage: triggered as a wrapper that opens/runs `FFTpro()`.

param_docs.ctrlName: Igor control name passed automatically by the panel callback.

category: FFT, QPI, Fourier Filters, Phase, and Lock-In

name: FFTpro
description: Interactive Igor procedure for FFT, QPI, phase, or lock-in analysis; symmetry or reflection processing; matrix resampling, scaling, or reshaping. Purpose: performs Fourier, filtering, phase, or lock-in processing. Usage: run from Igor with parameters `mat`, `number`, `start`, `a`, `theta`, `unit`. Dialog prompts: mat = Name of Map Batch; number = Number of the Batch; start = Start number of Batch; a = lattice constance (A); theta = angle between lattice and x+; unit = k-space unit. Code behavior: performs Fourier-transform or inverse-transform operations; creates output waves; duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output; opens or updates graph/image displays. Main internal calls: `FFTsymind()`, `di()`.

param_docs.mat: Name of Map Batch
param_docs.number: Number of the Batch

category: FFT, QPI, Fourier Filters, Phase, and Lock-In

name: FFTsymind
description: Function for FFT, QPI, phase, or lock-in analysis; symmetry or reflection processing; matrix resampling, scaling, or reshaping. Purpose: performs Fourier, filtering, phase, or lock-in processing. Usage: call as `FFTsymind(mat)`. Code behavior: creates output waves; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output.

param_docs.mat: Name or reference of the source wave/matrix being processed. In this context it is used for Fourier/QPI or phase-map processing.

category: FFT, QPI, Fourier Filters, Phase, and Lock-In

name: LF
description: Main Function Usage: call as `LF(name, qx1, qy1, qx2, qy2, avedia, condA, condB)`. Code behavior: performs Fourier-transform or inverse-transform operations; creates output waves; duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; reshapes existing waves; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `unpadding()`, `atan2()`, `cnfyj()`. Usage: Update-log guidance - 2023-07-13: Lawler-Fujita drift correction using the same Launch/GetA/GetB workflow as the FFT filter. Later FFT Engineering updates use Q_A and Q_B from the interpolated FFT image and support marquee-based fitting limits.

name: PhaseMap
description: Interactive Igor procedure for Nanonis/STM data loading or map conversion; FFT, QPI, phase, or lock-in analysis; spectroscopy, superconducting-gap, or vortex-model analysis. Purpose: performs Fourier, filtering, phase, or lock-in processing. Usage: run from Igor with parameters `name1`, `name2`, `size1`, `x1`, `y1`, `x2`, `y2`. Dialog prompts: name1 = name of the gap map; name2 = name of the topography; size1 = dimsize of the subwindow (must be even number, but I write a correction code inside, you do not need to care about the even odd here); x1 = x of FFT Point 1; y1 = y of FFT Point 1; x2 = x of FFT Point 2. Code behavior: performs Fourier-transform or inverse-transform operations; asks the user for parameters through Igor prompts. Main internal calls: `makechunkdataFFT()`.

param_docs.size1: dimsize of the subwindow (must be even number, but I write a correction code inside, you do not need to care about the even odd here)

category: FFT, QPI, Fourier Filters, Phase, and Lock-In

name: makechunkdataFFT
description: Utility function for Nanonis/STM data loading or map conversion; FFT, QPI, phase, or lock-in analysis; matrix resampling, scaling, or reshaping. Purpose: creates new waves, maps, figures, or simulation data. Usage: call as `makechunkdataFFT(name1, name2, size1, x1, y1, x2, y2)`. Code behavior: performs Fourier-transform or inverse-transform operations; creates output waves; duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output; opens or updates graph/image displays. Main internal calls: `twoDinterpolatexyfd()`, `margin()`.

param_docs.size1: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.

param_docs.y1: Start point, index, or coordinate for the selected range. In this context it is used for Fourier/QPI or phase-map processing.
param_docs.x2: End point, index, or coordinate for the selected range. In this context it is used for Fourier/QPI or phase-map processing.

name: automatrixTC
description: Interactive Igor procedure for Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Usage: run from Igor with parameters `paralist`, `xyzseq`, `sel`. Dialog prompts: paralist = List (by;) of parameter waves (1x1 waves); xyzseq = Wave of Pauli sequence, e.g. a polynomial with 6 terms, each term has 3 paulis, the wave should be like {{0,1,2},{2,3,1},{0,2,2},{1,1,1},{2,2,2},{1,0,0}}; sel = Select Modes. Code behavior: asks the user for parameters through Igor prompts. Main internal calls: `automatrixT()`, `automatrixC()`.

param_docs.xyzseq: Wave of Pauli sequence, e.g. a polynomial with 6 terms, each term has 3 paulis, the wave should be like {{0,1,2},{2,3,1},{0,2,2},{1,1,1},{2,2,2},{1,0,0}}

category: Matrix, Cube, Wave, and Batch Operations

name: automatrixT
description: # 02. Main Function for Text wave [This useful for derivation equation] Usage: call as `automatrixT(paralist, xyzseq)`. Code behavior: duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `mpt()`, `st0()`, `stx()`, `sty()`, `stz()`, `plust()`. Update log: 10/18/2023: Debugs for automatrixC() automatrixT() Previous version cannot be applicable to Hamiltonian with only one Pauli matrix (2x2), the problem solved now. 10/12/2023: (1) automatrixT(paralist,xyzseq): deriving the text wave matrix equation, the paralist is the name list of parameter waves, xyzseq is a real numerical wave containing the Pauli matrix sequence.

param_docs.paralist: String name used to locate an Igor wave, graph, folder, or output object.

category: Matrix, Cube, Wave, and Batch Operations

name: automatrixC
description: # 02. Main Function for complex numerical wave [This useful for model calculation in function] Usage: call as `automatrixC(paralist, xyzseq)`. Code behavior: duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `mpc()`, `s0()`, `sx()`, `sy()`, `sz()`. Update log: 10/18/2023: Debugs for automatrixC() automatrixT() Previous version cannot be applicable to Hamiltonian with only one Pauli matrix (2x2), the problem solved now. 10/12/2023: (2) automatrixC(paralist,$xyzseq): deriving the complex numerical wave.

param_docs.paralist: String name used to locate an Igor wave, graph, folder, or output object.

Usage: Update-log guidance - 08/17/2023: The old version was created on early 2021 and was modified until late of 2022, the old version is functional by sequentially running (1) getslicerData(name,a,b,c,xs,ys,Es,Ed) (for slicing a 3D matrix <name>, to be multiple 2D matrix), (2) mapforSTMf(num,name,suff,start,delta) (combining them together to a 3D matrix again, (3) makeextra(), (launch the smarter plot). This is a stupid algorithm owing to retro back to early year, I am trying to make use of some well written procedures in the General ARPES template, in order to make them compatible, I need to slice every layer to be 2D image first. **The slicer may be potentially useful in the future getslicerData(name,a,b,c,xs,ys,Es,Ed).

description: duplicate/o Root:Grid001:Grid001_LI_Demod_1_Y Root:mat3draw Purpose: extracts values, metadata, cursor information, or derived waves. Usage: run from Igor with parameters `name`, `a`, `b`, `c`, `xs`, `ys`, `Es`, `Ed`. Dialog prompts: name = Name of the 3D matrix; a = X is which index in Matrix? Row (0); Coloum (1); Layer (2); b = Y is which index in Matrix? Row (0); Coloum (1); Layer (2); c = E is which index in Matrix? Row (0); Coloum (1); Layer (2); xs = x-size; ys = y-size. Code behavior: duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output; asks the user for parameters through Igor prompts. Main internal calls: `getslicerDataf()`. Usage: Update-log guidance - 08/17/2023: The old version was created on early 2021 and was modified until late of 2022, the old version is functional by sequentially running (1) getslicerData(name,a,b,c,xs,ys,Es,Ed) (for slicing a 3D matrix <name>, to be multiple 2D matrix), (2) mapforSTMf(num,name,suff,start,delta) (combining them together to a 3D matrix again, (3) makeextra(), (launch the smarter plot). This is a stupid algorithm owing to retro back to early year, I am trying to make use of some well written procedures in the General ARPES template, in order to make them compatible, I need to slice every layer to be 2D image first. **The slicer may be potentially useful in the future getslicerData(name,a,b,c,xs,ys,Es,Ed). Update log: 08/17/2023: **The slicer may be potentially useful in the future getslicerData(name,a,b,c,xs,ys,Es,Ed) ##[Features of the new version].

param_docs.c: E is which index in Matrix? Row (0); Coloum (1); Layer (2)

description: Function for linecut, slice, or region extraction; Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Usage: call as `manualvalue_1Dextrac(leftvalue, rightvalue)`. Main internal calls: `str2num()`. Usage: Update-log guidance - 2024-06-27: Added manual correction for MultiPeak 1D extraction. Use `manualvalue_1Dextrac(leftvalue, rightvalue)` from the command line or panel button; each argument is a semicolon-separated list. A blank value writes NaN for a missing peak, `0` leaves the existing peak unchanged, and the entries are ordered by increasing absolute peak energy.

name: Deriv
signature: function Deriv(OldM,num,box,choice) //command line of 2nd derivative, the same as main panel.
description: Peng Zhang curvature Usage: call as `Deriv(OldM, num, box, choice)`. Code behavior: duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing. Main internal calls: `DM()`.

param_docs.OldM: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.
param_docs.num: Number of spectra, waves, points, bins, or iterations to process.
param_docs.box: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.
param_docs.choice: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.

name: curvature
signature: function curvature(mat,num,box,choice,factor) //curvature. 1 is EDC, 2 is MDC, 3 is 2D with unit problem, 4 is the method that using the max between EDC and MDC(not efficient).
description: Function for graph display, formatting, or window management; Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Usage: call as `curvature(mat, num, box, choice, factor)`. Code behavior: duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; opens or updates graph/image displays; formats graph axes, labels, colors, or annotations; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `DM1()`, `max()`, `wintype()`. Update log: 2021-09-17: Signal-enhancement routine based on Peng Zhang's curvature-analysis method for highlighting dispersive features.

param_docs.mat: Name or reference of the source wave/matrix being processed.
param_docs.num: Number of spectra, waves, points, bins, or iterations to process.
param_docs.box: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.
param_docs.choice: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.
param_docs.factor: Scaling, normalization, interpolation, or correction factor.

name: curvature2
signature: function curvature2(mat,num,box,num2,box2,choice,factor) //there are two groups of smooth parameters compared to curvature(). choice are 3 and 4.
description: Function for graph display, formatting, or window management; Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Usage: call as `curvature2(mat, num, box, num2, box2, choice, factor)`. Code behavior: duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing. Main internal calls: `DM1()`, `max()`.

param_docs.mat: Name or reference of the source wave/matrix being processed.
param_docs.num: Number of spectra, waves, points, bins, or iterations to process.
param_docs.box: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.
param_docs.num2: Number of spectra, waves, points, bins, or iterations to process.
param_docs.box2: End point, index, or coordinate for the selected range.
param_docs.choice: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.
param_docs.factor: Scaling, normalization, interpolation, or correction factor.

param_docs.NewM: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.

param_docs.choice: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.

description: End of Movie maker, by Jonathan Henry Bowen Purpose: fits or extracts spectral/peak parameters. Usage: call as `GoldFitting(dos, Tem)`. Code behavior: runs fitting routines on spectra, curves, or map-derived traces; creates output waves; formats graph axes, labels, colors, or annotations. Main internal calls: `csrinfo()`, `hcsr()`, `vcsr()`, `dos()`.

param_docs.dos: Used in: CurveFit/Q line, dos(2,startx)/D// the result return in a wave by default w_coef / CurveFit/Q line, dos(endx,18)/D
param_docs.Tem: Temperature parameter, usually in kelvin when used in spectral modeling.

category: Spectroscopy, Gap, Peak, and Fitting Workflows

name: GoldFittingp
signature: function GoldFittingp(dos,Tem) //the same with fitgold(dos,Tem), only different is user need to guess all the initial values of the six parameters.
description: Function for spectroscopy, superconducting-gap, or vortex-model analysis; ARPES-style loading, plotting, or momentum conversion; Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Purpose: fits or extracts spectral/peak parameters. Usage: call as `GoldFittingp(dos, Tem)`. Dialog prompts: Wfit0 = fermi level; Wfit1 = fitting temperature; Wfit2 = below Ef line's Y value when x=0; Wfit3 = the slope of the below Ef line; Wfit4 = above Ef line's Y value when x=0; Wfit5 = the slope of the above Ef line. Code behavior: runs fitting routines on spectra, curves, or map-derived traces; creates output waves; duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; extracts image/wave statistics; differentiates wave data. Main internal calls: `csrinfo()`, `hcsr()`, `vcsr()`, `dos()`.

param_docs.dos: Used in: FuncFit/Q line_fermi Wfit dos(startx, endx) /D / FuncFit/Q line_fermi Wfit dos(startx, endx) /D
param_docs.Tem: Temperature parameter, usually in kelvin when used in spectral modeling.

category: FFT, QPI, Fourier Filters, Phase, and Lock-In

name: C4sheargetparac

Purpose: applies geometric correction or extracts deformation/strain information. Usage: run from Igor with parameters `alphaini`, `ppini`, `thetaini`, `qq`, `hold`. Dialog prompts: alphaini = α (Ideal vector angle from +x in degree); ppini = P (X shear parameter); thetaini = θ (Shear axis angle from +x in degree); qq = |q| (vector norm of the ideal lattice. ##Note## this should be real value, the length directly read from FFT should be multipied by 2pi. #This value affects only choose Hold |q| and put a non-zero value, other wise, the |q| will be automatically given by q1 and q2#); hold = Hold ideal |q| ?. Code behavior: performs Fourier-transform or inverse-transform operations; formats graph axes, labels, colors, or a

param_docs.alphaini: α (Ideal vector angle from +x in degree)
param_docs.thetaini: θ (Shear axis angle from +x in degree)
param_docs.qq: |q| (vector norm of the ideal lattice. ##Note## this should be real value, the length directly read from FFT should be multipied by 2pi. #This value affects only choose Hold |q| and put a non-zero value, other wise, the |q| will be automatically given by q1 and q2#)

Purpose: applies geometric correction or extracts deformation/strain information. Usage: call as `shearall(mat3dn, refwave, factor)`. Code behavior: creates output waves; duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; reshapes existing waves; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output. Main internal calls: `func_zeroNaN()`. Update log: 2023-09-12: Applies shear correction to every layer of a 3D matrix through the "Go3D" workflow. name: d3d
description: Interactive Igor procedure for linecut, slice, or region extraction; matrix resampling, scaling, or reshaping; smoothing, normalization, or background removal. Usage: run from Igor with parameters `mat3dn`, `zn`. Dialog prompts: mat3dn = Name of the; zn = Index of the Energy. Code behavior: performs Fourier-transform or inverse-transform operations; creates output waves; duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output; opens or updates graph/image displays. Main internal calls: `getall3dwave()`, `sumoned()`, `di2lf()`, `func_zeroNaN()`, `color3s_for3d()`, `stringmatch()`. Usage: Update-log guidance - 2023-08-17: Added the smarter 3D matrix plotter. Launch it with `d3d("name", zn)`, where `zn` is the energy dimension, usually 2. The viewer shows a constant-energy image, an integrated f(Z) trace, and an FFT image; changing Z updates the image and FFT, the Linecut button opens horizontal/vertical/arbitrary linecut tools, and optional Z normalization keeps a reversible `Raw_name` copy. 2023-08-17: The viewer can feed FFT engineering: activate the constant-Z image, click Launch in the FFT Engineer section, then tune the Z plane while using 2D lock-in or FFT-filter workflows. MDC slicing can convert selected linecuts into waterfall traces for Z_color display.
category: Smart Display, Graph Formatting, and Figure Tools

Main internal calls: `getall3dwave()`, `sumoned()`.
category: Spectroscopy, Gap, Peak, and Fitting Workflows

name: sumoned
description: Function for FFT, QPI, phase, or lock-in analysis; linecut, slice, or region extraction; Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Purpose: combines many waves/slices into a map, linecut, or averaged output. Usage: call as `sumoned(dest, orin, zn)`. Code behavior: creates output waves; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output; extracts image/wave statistics; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `tpw()`, `mean()`. Update log: 10/03/2023: (7) Upgrade the sumoned() function for sum over the Grid to get average 1D wave. Now it can apply to a Grid with NaN value, the procedure will skip that 1D curve while counting the averages. Roughly finalized Lattice Segregation. 08/17/2023: New functions mounted, Normalinecut(name) to normalize 2D linecut along x direction. sumoned(dest,orin,zn) to calculate averaged Z curve from a 3D matrix. grabwinnonew(name) can grab the winname of a wave without display a new window.

param_docs.zn: Index of your interesting dimension that keep in the end.

category: Smart Display, Graph Formatting, and Figure Tools

name: Z_R_Rhomap
description: Function for Nanonis/STM data loading or map conversion; symmetry or reflection processing; linecut, slice, or region extraction. Usage: call as `Z_R_Rhomap(wg, wi, zn_consl)`. Code behavior: creates output waves; duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output; extracts image/wave statistics; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `gammaNoise()`.

param_docs.wg: Igor wave reference used as input or output. In this context it usually refers to STM/STS or Nanonis-derived data.
param_docs.wi: Igor wave reference used as input or output. In this context it usually refers to STM/STS or Nanonis-derived data.
param_docs.zn_consl: Layer/index value selecting a slice of a 3D wave or energy stack. In this context it usually refers to STM/STS or Nanonis-derived data.

name: ButtonProc_autogatemap
description: Auto Gate map Panel control(s): `Auto / GateMp`. Usage: triggered as a wrapper that opens/runs `gatemapauto()`.

param_docs.ctrlName: Igor control name passed automatically by the panel callback.

name: gatemapauto
description: Interactive Igor procedure for Nanonis/STM data loading or map conversion; symmetry or reflection processing; linecut, slice, or region extraction. Usage: run from Igor with parameters `x`, `y`, `gate`, `evenornot`, `Interpnum`. Dialog prompts: x = Column Number of x wave; y = Column Number of y wave; gate = Column number for gate; evenornot = Gate even spacing?; Interpnum = If gate is not even spacing, how many points you want to interpolate. Code behavior: creates output waves; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output; formats graph axes, labels, colors, or annotations; creates, switches, or removes Igor data folders; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `Makegraphtable()`, `LoadDataFromNewmkgate()`, `Initialize_Global_Variables()`, `linkstsmapgate()`, `rescalexmultiwhat()`, `normrange()`. Update log: 07/29/2023: Added Auto gate map gatemapauto(), and improved the UnevenY by removing redundant waves.

param_docs.x: Column Number of x wave
param_docs.Interpnum: If gate is not even spacing, how many points you want to interpolate

name: LoadDataFromNewmkgate
description: Interactive Igor procedure for Nanonis/STM data loading or map conversion; linecut, slice, or region extraction; graph display, formatting, or window management. Purpose: loads data or launches an automatic loading workflow. Usage: run from Igor with parameters `x`, `y`, `gate`. Code behavior: duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; loads data from files or paths; creates, switches, or removes Igor data folders; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `IndexedFile()`, `wintype()`, `winType()`, `sortlist()`.

param_docs.x: Used in: LoadWave/G/D/A=load/K=1/L={0,0,0,x,1}/o/P=$PATHName Filename

name: linkstsmapgate
description: Function for Nanonis/STM data loading or map conversion; linecut, slice, or region extraction; matrix resampling, scaling, or reshaping. Purpose: combines many waves/slices into a map, linecut, or averaged output. Usage: call as `linkstsmapgate(name, num, startnum)`. Code behavior: creates output waves; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward.

param_docs.name: Name or reference of the source wave/matrix being processed. In this context it usually refers to STM/STS or Nanonis-derived data.
param_docs.num: Number of spectra, waves, points, bins, or iterations to process. In this context it usually refers to STM/STS or Nanonis-derived data.
param_docs.startnum: Start point, index, or coordinate for the selected range.

name: linkstsmapgl
description: Function for Nanonis/STM data loading or map conversion; linecut, slice, or region extraction; matrix resampling, scaling, or reshaping. Purpose: combines many waves/slices into a map, linecut, or averaged output. Usage: call as `linkstsmapgl(name, num, startnum)`. Code behavior: creates output waves; assigns Igor axis scaling/units to the output.

param_docs.name: Name or reference of the source wave/matrix being processed. In this context it usually refers to STM/STS or Nanonis-derived data.
param_docs.num: Number of spectra, waves, points, bins, or iterations to process. In this context it usually refers to STM/STS or Nanonis-derived data.
param_docs.startnum: Start point, index, or coordinate for the selected range.

name: ButtonProc_autotopo
description: Extract Topo after KP loads.sxm/.nsp. The data even number are bwd, and odd number are fwd Panel control(s): `Topo`. Usage: triggered as a wrapper that opens/runs `ExtacttopoafterKM()`.

param_docs.ctrlName: Igor control name passed automatically by the panel callback.

name: ExtacttopoafterKM
description: Interactive Igor procedure for Nanonis/STM data loading or map conversion; linecut, slice, or region extraction; Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Usage: run interactively from Igor or from a panel callback. Code behavior: duplicates or stages waves for downstream processing; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `DataFolderList()`, `KP_LoadNanonisData()`.

name: SaveGraph2
description: Utility function for graph display, formatting, or window management; Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Purpose: captures a graph recreation command so the graph can be rebuilt later. Usage: call `SaveGraph2()` from Igor procedure code or the command line. Code behavior: creates, switches, or removes Igor data folders; cleans up waves/windows or brings an existing window forward. Main internal calls: `WinRecreation()`, `SetWaveNameinProcText()`.
category: Smart Display, Graph Formatting, and Figure Tools

name: SetWaveNameinProcText
description: String-returning helper for graph display, formatting, or window management; Igor wave/matrix/cube data operation. Purpose: rewrites wave names inside stored graph recreation/procedure text. Usage: call as `SetWaveNameinProcText(wList, procText)`.

param_docs.wList: String name used to locate an Igor wave, graph, folder, or output object.
param_docs.procText: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.

category: Matrix, Cube, Wave, and Batch Operations

name: LoadGraph
description: Utility function for linecut, slice, or region extraction; graph display, formatting, or window management. Purpose: rebuilds a saved graph from stored recreation/procedure text. Usage: call `LoadGraph()` from Igor procedure code or the command line. Code behavior: opens or updates graph/image displays; writes or exports data; creates, switches, or removes Igor data folders. Main internal calls: `GetScrapText()`, `RemoveSingleQuote()`, `RebuildAppendCmd()`.
category: Smart Display, Graph Formatting, and Figure Tools

name: RemoveSingleQuote
description: String-returning helper for graph display, formatting, or window management. Purpose: removes quote characters from a stored graph command string before execution. Usage: call as `RemoveSingleQuote(s0)`.

param_docs.s0: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.

category: Smart Display, Graph Formatting, and Figure Tools

name: RebuildAppendCmd
description: String-returning helper for graph display, formatting, or window management. Purpose: rebuilds Igor append commands so saved graph traces point to the current waves. Usage: call as `RebuildAppendCmd(cmd)`.

param_docs.cmd: Numeric parameter controlling the calculation branch, index, range, scale, or model value.

category: Smart Display, Graph Formatting, and Figure Tools

name: TransferGraph
description: Igor menu definition for graph display, formatting, or window management. Usage: defines Igor menu entries; users normally access it from the Igor menu bar.
category: Smart Display, Graph Formatting, and Figure Tools
